$doc: ailoop backlog for ampere. Readiness/batches computed by schedule.mjs, never by eye.
Append-mostly; never delete tickets. files is a contract enforced by the verifier.
Workers read SPEC.md and .ailoop/oracle.md in the repo; contexts cite sections.

tickets.title: SPM package scaffold with all five targets
tickets.context: Greenfield repo at ~/Projects/ampere (SPEC.md + docs/ exist, no Swift code). Create the SPM package per SPEC §2, exactly: swift-tools-version 6.0; targets AmpereCore (library), ampered (executable), Ampere (executable), ampere-cli (executable), AmpereCoreTests (Swift Testing, depends on AmpereCore); every target gets swiftSettings: [.swiftLanguageMode(.v5)]; platforms [.macOS(.v14)]. Each executable main is a compilable stub (print a version line sourced from AmpereCore Version.swift; Ampere's stub may be a plain main.swift-style entry for now — the real MenuBarExtra comes in T011, keep the file named AmpereApp.swift with a minimal @main struct that just runs). .gitignore: .build/, dist/, .DS_Store, *.xcodeproj. This is pure scaffold — exempt from the new-tests rule, but include one trivial Swift Testing test (Version string non-empty) so `swift test` exercises the test target.
tickets.acceptance:
- `swift build` exit 0
- `swift test` exit 0 with the Version test passing
- `swift package describe --type json` lists targets AmpereCore, ampered, Ampere, ampere-cli, AmpereCoreTests
- `.build/debug/ampere-cli` runs and prints a version line
tickets.evidence: re-verify 2026-07-05: swift build exit 0; bash scripts/test.sh -> 1/1 passed (Swift Testing per amendment 0004); swift package describe targets = Ampere, AmpereCore, AmpereCoreTests, ampere-cli, ampered; .build/debug/ampere-cli -> "ampere-cli 0.0.1"; scope clean (only contracted files)

tickets.title: SMC IOKit client in AmpereCore (port from vendored bclm-SMC.swift)
tickets.context: Port docs/reference/bclm-SMC.swift (SMCKit, MIT — KEEP the MIT license header in the ported file) into Sources/AmpereCore/SMC.swift as public API. Required surface: open/close connection to the AppleSMC IOService; readData(key) -> (bytes, type, size); writeData(key, bytes); keyInformation(key) -> (type, size) or nil if the key doesn't exist; a FourCharCode conversion for 4-char string keys. Trim what we don't need (fan/temperature convenience APIs) but keep the 80-byte SMCParamStruct and the IOConnectCallStructMethod selector-2 plumbing intact. Do NOT hardcode any charging keys here — this is a generic client; the allowlist enforcement comes later (SPEC §4). No writes are performed in tests (tests must run without root and without touching hardware).
tickets.acceptance:
- unit test: fourCharCode("CHTE") == 0x43485445 and fourCharCode("CH0B") == 0x43483042, and decoding back yields the strings
- unit test: MemoryLayout<SMCParamStruct>.size == 80
- unit test: constructing a read/write param struct for a known key sets the key field and data size correctly (inspect struct fields, contrast read vs write command bytes)
- `swift build` + `swift test` green
tickets.evidence: re-verify 2026-07-05 on worktree: scope = exactly [SMC.swift, SMCTests.swift]; build exit 0; scripts/test.sh 10/10; MIT header kept (line 17); selectors 2/5/6/9 + 80-byte SMCParamStruct match vendored reference; no charging keys hardcoded (generic client). Merged to main.

tickets.title: BatteryState model + AppleSmartBattery reader with injectable parser
tickets.context: Two pieces. (1) BatteryState.swift: the frozen struct from SPEC §3.3 (percent, isCharging, externalConnected, temperatureC) plus an extended BatteryReading that also carries cycleCount, appleRawMaxCapacity, designCapacity, amperageMA, voltageMV (SPEC §4 key list). (2) BatteryReader.swift: parse(_ dict: [String: Any]) -> BatteryReading — pure, injectable for tests — plus a live path that fetches the AppleSmartBattery IORegistry service dictionary via IOServiceGetMatchingService/IORegistryEntryCreateCFProperties (no root needed). Temperature key is centi-degrees C: 3011 -> 30.11. Missing keys -> sensible defaults + a `complete: Bool` flag, never a crash.
tickets.acceptance:
- fixture test A (charging): dict {CurrentCapacity:62, IsCharging:true, ExternalConnected:true, Temperature:3011, CycleCount:412, AppleRawMaxCapacity:4382, DesignCapacity:5088, Amperage:1250, Voltage:12600} parses to percent 62, charging true, temperatureC 30.11, watts computable
- fixture test B (discharging): IsCharging:false, ExternalConnected:false, Amperage:-890 parses with negative amperage and externalConnected false — outputs must differ from A in exactly those fields
- fixture test C: empty dict -> complete == false, no crash
- `swift build` + `swift test` green
tickets.evidence: re-verify 2026-07-05 on worktree: scope = exactly the 3 contracted files; swift build exit 0; scripts/test.sh 5/5 (fixtures A/B/C incl. contrast + partial-dict); gaming read clean (pure parse, centi-C /100, complete flag per missing key, readLive falls back to parse([:])). Merged to main.

tickets.title: Config model (Codable, defaults, clamping, persistence helpers)
tickets.context: Config exactly per SPEC §3.2: limitPercent(80), sailingEnabled(false), sailingOffset(5), heatProtectionEnabled(true), heatThresholdC(35.0), calibrationScheduleEnabled(false), calibrationDayOfMonth(1), mode("limit" | "off"). All fields must decode with defaults when absent (decodeIfPresent pattern) so old config files never fail. Provide load(from url:)/save(to url:) helpers (atomic write) and a `settingLimit(_:)` that clamps to 50...100. Do not touch /Library paths in tests — tests use temp dir URLs.
tickets.acceptance:
- decode test: `{}` yields every default listed above
- decode test: `{"limitPercent": 60}` yields 60 with other defaults intact (contrast with previous)
- clamp test: settingLimit(101) == 100, settingLimit(10) == 50, settingLimit(75) == 75
- round-trip: save then load in temp dir preserves all fields
- `swift build` + `swift test` green
tickets.evidence: re-verify 2026-07-05 on worktree: scope = exactly [Config.swift, ConfigTests.swift]; swift build exit 0; scripts/test.sh 6/6 passed (5 ConfigTests incl. empty-object defaults, partial override, clamp 101/10/75, round-trip); gaming read clean (decodeIfPresent per field, clamp 50...100, atomic save). Merged to main.

tickets.title: Socket protocol codec (JSON lines, request/response types)
tickets.context: Codable request/response types + line codec for the protocol EXACTLY as SPEC §3.1: cmds get-state, set-limit(value), set-config(partial config merge semantics defined here as: only provided fields override), get-config, action(name: discharge-to-limit|top-up|calibrate-start|calibrate-abort), get-stats(hours). Response envelope {ok, data?/error?}. get-state's data payload struct includes: percent, isCharging, externalConnected, chargingPaused, pauseReason (nil|"limit"|"heat"), adapterDisabled, mode, limit, temperatureC, health{maxCapacity,designCapacity,cycleCount}, calibration (nil or {phase, startedAt}). Pure codec only — no sockets in this ticket (server/client are T009). Unknown cmd decodes to a case that encodes an {ok:false} error response.
tickets.acceptance:
- encode/decode round-trip test for every cmd with distinct payloads (contrast: set-limit 65 vs 80 decode to different values)
- unknown cmd line `{"cmd":"bogus"}` -> error response with ok:false and non-empty error
- get-state payload with pauseReason heat vs limit encode to different JSON (assert both strings)
- `swift build` + `swift test` green
tickets.evidence: re-verify 2026-07-05 on worktree: scope exact [Protocol.swift, ProtocolTests.swift]; build exit 0; 39/39 tests (20 new ProtocolTests: per-cmd round-trips w/ contrast, unknown-cmd error, heat-vs-limit pauseReason JSON contrast). Merged to main; merged gate green.

tickets.title: Pure control core: decide() for limit/sailing/heat/discharge/top-up
tickets.context: THE core ticket. Implement SPEC §3.3 exactly: ChargingCommand enum, ControlState struct (current one-shot mode: none|discharging|toppingUp, plus lastCommands for idempotence bookkeeping; include a calibration placeholder field `calibration: CalibrationState?` typed but always nil for now — the calibration rules are a later ticket and will extend this file), and pure func decide(BatteryState, Config, ControlState, now: Date) -> (commands: [ChargingCommand], next: ControlState). Frozen decision rules are in SPEC §3.3 — implement the decision table literally, including: hysteresis (inhibit at >= limit, allow at <= limit-5 or limit-sailingOffset when sailing, in-between keeps previous), heat always wins (>= threshold forces inhibit, releases at threshold-2), discharge-to-limit with hard floor 20 (floor breach -> enableAdapter+allowCharging+revert to limit mode), top-up until >=100 or (!isCharging && >=99), externalConnected==false emits no hardware commands, mode==off emits allowCharging+enableAdapter once then nothing.
tickets.acceptance:
Contrast table tests, at minimum (limit 80, sailing off, heat off unless stated):
- percent 79 charging, no prior inhibit -> no inhibit command
- percent 80 -> inhibitCharging
- percent 78 after inhibit (between 75 and 80) -> keeps inhibited (no allow)
- percent 75 after inhibit -> allowCharging
- sailing on offset 10: allow only at <= 70
- temperatureC 36 threshold 35, percent 50 -> inhibitCharging (heat), then 33.5 -> release (33 < 35-2 boundary: release strictly at <= 33)
- discharging mode percent 81 limit 80 -> disableAdapter; percent 80 -> enableAdapter + mode back to limit; percent 19 (floor breach) -> enableAdapter + allowCharging + limit mode
- top-up: percent 99 isCharging false -> done; percent 99 isCharging true -> keep allowing
- externalConnected false -> commands empty
All as Swift Testing tests; `swift build` + `swift test` green
tickets.evidence: re-verify 2026-07-06: scope exact [ControlCore.swift, ControlCoreTests.swift]; build exit 0; 58/58 (19 new contrast tests: hysteresis 79/80/78/75, sailing 71/70, heat 36/33/34, discharge 81/80/19-floor, top-up, unplugged, off-mode, idempotence); code read faithful to SPEC §3.3 incl. heat early-return override and defensive adapter-enable in limit mode. Merged; gate green.

tickets.title: ampere-cli spike commands: keys / status / pause / resume / adapter
tickets.context: Extend ampere-cli (stub from T001) with subcommands per SPEC §5 Phase 0. Hand-rolled argument parsing (no dependencies — locked). `status`: read BatteryReader live (no root), print one JSON object {percent, isCharging, externalConnected, temperatureC, cycleCount, maxCapacity, designCapacity}. `keys`: for each allowlist key (CHTE, CH0B, CH0C, CHIE, CH0I — SPEC §4) print exists/type/size from SMC keyInformation (reads are unprivileged). `pause`/`resume`: write charging-inhibit per SPEC §4 fallback order (try CHTE first — probe type via key info and write 1/0 using the probed size; if CHTE missing, write CH0B AND CH0C 0x02/0x00). `adapter off|on`: CHIE 0x08/0x00, fallback CH0I 0x01/0x00. Root guard: geteuid() != 0 for any write command -> print clear error to stderr, exit 1, write nothing. After any write, read back and print the key's new value. IMPORTANT: never write any key outside the SPEC §4 allowlist; print exactly what was written for the human hardware gate.
tickets.acceptance:
- `swift build` green, `swift test` green (existing suite must not regress)
- `.build/debug/ampere-cli status` exits 0 and prints JSON whose percent is 0...100 (verifier cross-checks against `pmset -g batt` percentage ±1 — live contrast, not hardcodable)
- `.build/debug/ampere-cli keys` exits 0 (no sudo) and prints one line per allowlist key with an exists boolean and, when present, type/size from live key-info
- `.build/debug/ampere-cli pause` WITHOUT sudo exits 1 with error mentioning root; same for resume / adapter
(The sudo pause/resume/adapter behavior is the Phase 0 [HW] gate, run by the human — not this ticket's acceptance.)
tickets.evidence: re-verify 2026-07-05 on worktree + merged tree: scope exact [main.swift, SpikeCommands.swift]; build exit 0; 19/19 then 39/39 on merge; LIVE: status percent 85 == pmset 85; keys probe: CHTE ui32/4 exists, CHIE hex_/1 exists, CH0B/CH0C/CH0I absent; root guards exit 1 before any SMC open. Gaming read: every writeData site targets allowlist keys only.

tickets.title: SMC adapter (allowlist-enforced, verify-after-write) + ampered daemon loop
tickets.context: (1) SMCAdapter.swift: the ONLY module that turns ChargingCommands into SMC writes. API per SPEC §4: probe() caches which inhibit key (CHTE else CH0B/CH0C) and adapter key (CHIE else CH0I) this firmware has; setChargingInhibited(Bool)/setAdapterDisabled(Bool) write per SPEC §4 values, consult docs/smc-findings.md semantics (if that file exists, its confirmed values override the defaults — read it as documentation, hardcode the SPEC §4 defaults). Inject the low-level writer as a protocol (SMCWriting) so tests use a fake. After each write, read back battery state (injectable reader) and set a `lastWriteVerified: Bool` flag — a write that doesn't change IsCharging within one re-read marks unverified (firmware-change canary, surfaced later in get-state). Idempotence: repeated same-command calls write only once. (2) Daemon.swift + main.swift: event loop per SPEC §3 — 30 s DispatchSourceTimer poll, IOPSNotificationCreateRunLoopSource callback, IORegisterForSystemPower sleep/wake (on wake: immediate re-evaluate); each tick: read battery -> decide() (T006) -> apply commands via adapter; load config from SPEC §3 path at startup (create with defaults if absent). Signal handling per SPEC §3: SIGTERM/SIGINT -> setChargingInhibited(false), setAdapterDisabled(false), exit 0. No socket server yet (T009/T010). Daemon refuses to run if geteuid() != 0.
tickets.acceptance:
- SMCAdapterTests with a fake SMCWriting: allowlist enforcement (attempting a non-allowlist key traps/asserts in debug — test via the public API only exposing the two setters); idempotence (two setChargingInhibited(true) calls -> exactly one write recorded); fallback (fake reports CHTE missing -> writes go to CH0B AND CH0C); verify-after-write flag flips when fake reader disagrees (contrast: agreeing reader -> verified true)
- `swift build` + `swift test` green
- `.build/debug/ampered` run as non-root exits non-zero with a clear message
tickets.evidence: re-verify 2026-07-06: scope exact (4 contracted files); 82/82 worktree, 97/97 merged; non-root refusal exit 1; byte-exactness vs docs/smc-findings.md confirmed in source (CHTE LE, CHIE 0x08, CH0B/C 0x02 both); key-literal audit: only 5 allowlist keys in file; SIGTERM/SIGINT restore charging+adapter then exit 0. Merged; gate green.

tickets.title: Unix socket server (handler-based) + client library + loopback tests
tickets.context: Plain POSIX unix-domain stream sockets (socket/bind/listen/accept + DispatchSource or a background thread; no third-party code). SocketServer: init(path:, mode: mode_t, handler: (RequestLine) -> ResponseLine) — accepts connections, reads newline-delimited requests, writes one response line per request; start()/stop(); stop() unlinks the socket file. SocketClient: connect(path:), request(_ line:) -> line (with timeout). Both are generic over the T005 codec (server takes a closure so it has NO dependency on daemon internals — the daemon wires it in T010). chmod the socket to the given mode after bind; ownership (root:staff per SPEC §3) happens naturally when the daemon runs as root — do not chown in code.
tickets.acceptance:
- Swift Testing test binds a REAL unix socket at a NSTemporaryDirectory() path, echoes through the T005 codec: send get-state request -> handler returns a canned state -> client decodes it (assert a field value)
- error path test: malformed line -> ok:false response, connection stays usable for the next request (contrast: valid request after malformed still succeeds)
- stop() removes the socket file (assert FileManager non-existence)
- `swift build` + `swift test` green
tickets.evidence: re-verify 2026-07-06: scope exact [SocketServer, SocketClient, SocketTests]; build exit 0; 46/46 twice (flake check); real AF_UNIX socket calls confirmed; stale unlink + chmod(mode) after bind; handler-closure design (no daemon coupling). Merged; merged gate green.

tickets.title: Wire socket into daemon + ampere-cli install/uninstall/state
tickets.context: (1) DaemonServer.swift: instantiate T009's SocketServer at /var/run/ampere.sock mode 0660, handler implements every SPEC §3.1 cmd against the live daemon state (get-state assembles the full payload incl. pauseReason and lastWriteVerified canary as `writeVerified`; set-limit/set-config mutate config + persist + immediate re-evaluate; action dispatches one-shot modes into ControlState; get-stats returns [] until T014). (2) LaunchdPlist.swift in AmpereCore: pure function returning the plist XML string for label com.ampere.daemon, ProgramArguments [/Library/PrivilegedHelperTools/ampered], RunAtLoad true, KeepAlive true — pure so it's unit-testable. (3) InstallCommands.swift: `install` (root-guarded): copy .build's ampered (or --binary path) to /Library/PrivilegedHelperTools/ampered (create dir 0755), write plist to /Library/LaunchDaemons/com.ampere.daemon.plist, `launchctl bootstrap system <plist>`; `uninstall`: `launchctl bootout system/com.ampere.daemon`, remove plist + binary + socket, leave config; both support `--dry-run` printing each planned action without executing (no root needed for dry-run). `state`: SocketClient get-state, pretty-print (no root). Keep T007's spike commands working.
tickets.acceptance:
- PlistTests: generated plist contains label com.ampere.daemon, the binary path, RunAtLoad true AND `plutil -lint` accepts it (write to temp file in test)
- `.build/debug/ampere-cli install --dry-run` exits 0 (no sudo) and prints, in order: binary copy destination, plist path, bootstrap command — assert all three substrings
- `.build/debug/ampere-cli uninstall --dry-run` prints bootout command + both removal paths
- `swift build` + `swift test` green
([HW] install/limit/sleep/uninstall checks are the Phase 1 gate, human-run.)
tickets.evidence: re-verify 2026-07-06: scope exact (7 files); 100/100 worktree+merged; PlistTests use real plutil; dry-runs print exact install/uninstall actions; state exits 1 cleanly daemon-absent; spike commands unchanged.

tickets.title: MenuBarExtra app shell + daemon client view model + status glyph logic
tickets.context: Replace T001's Ampere stub with a real SwiftUI MenuBarExtra app (SPEC §2): @main App with MenuBarExtra(.window style), NSApp.setActivationPolicy(.accessory) at launch. DaemonClientModel: ObservableObject polling get-state via SocketClient every 5 s while the popover is closed-cheap (timer) and immediately on open; gracefully handles daemon-absent (connection refused -> state .daemonUnavailable). MenuBarView: menu bar label shows percent + glyph; popover shows current state summary and (until T012) a placeholder section. StatusFormatting.swift in AmpereCore (pure, tested): func glyph(for state) -> String over SF Symbol names — charging -> bolt variants, paused-at-limit -> pause variant, discharging(adapter off) -> distinct glyph, daemonUnavailable -> exclamation variant; func label(percent) -> "75%". Keep it small; controls land in T012.
tickets.acceptance:
- StatusFormattingTests: four contrasting states (charging / paused limit / adapter-off discharge / daemon unavailable) map to FOUR DISTINCT symbol names; label(75) == "75%"
- `swift build` + `swift test` green
- `.build/debug/Ampere` launches without crashing when the daemon socket is absent (verifier: run with a 5 s timeout, expect it still alive when killed, exit via signal — no crash log)
tickets.evidence: re-verify 2026-07-06: scope exact (5 contracted files); build exit 0; 72/72 (7 new StatusFormattingTests incl. pairwise-distinct glyph set); app launches and stays alive 3s with socket absent, clean SIGTERM; DaemonClientModel @MainActor with Task.detached socket I/O, defaults to daemonUnavailable. Merged; gate green.

tickets.title: Controls popover: limit slider, sailing toggle, discharge/top-up actions
tickets.context: ControlsView inside the popover (wired into MenuBarView's placeholder from T011): limit Slider 50...100 step 5 with live % label -> set-limit on release (debounced, not per-tick); sailing Toggle + offset Stepper (5...20) -> set-config; buttons 'Discharge to limit' and 'Top up to 100%' -> action cmds, disabled when daemon unavailable or already in that one-shot mode (read from get-state mode field); an 'Off' toggle mapping to config.mode off|limit with a caution footnote. All mutations go through DaemonClientModel (T011) — no new socket code. Show pauseReason ('Paused: heat') and the writeVerified canary ('⚠︎ charge control may be broken by a macOS update') when false.

- `swift build` + `swift test` green (no regressions)

- `.build/debug/Ampere` still launches daemon-absent without crash
tickets.evidence: re-verify 2026-07-06: scope exact (3 files); 97/97 worktree, 100/100 merged; contract greps: zero SocketClient() in views, slider sends on onEditingChanged(false), heat row uses live temperatureC, writeVerified warning present; app alive 3s daemon-absent.

tickets.title: make-app.sh bundle script, launch-at-login, first-run install prompt, README
tickets.context: (1) scripts/make-app.sh: swift build -c release, assemble dist/Ampere.app (Contents/MacOS/Ampere from release binary, Contents/Info.plist with CFBundleIdentifier com.ampere.app, CFBundleName Ampere, LSUIElement true, LSMinimumSystemVersion 14.0), `codesign -s - --force dist/Ampere.app`. Idempotent (rm -rf dist/Ampere.app first). (4) README.md: what Ampere is, build steps, install/uninstall, the SPEC §8 warning that macOS Settings > Battery charge limit must stay off/100%, and the firmware-canary explanation.
tickets.acceptance:

tickets.evidence: re-verify 2026-07-06: scope exact (5 files); make-app.sh produces signed dist/Ampere.app (plutil lint OK, LSUIElement true, codesign -v OK, ampered+ampere-cli bundled in Resources); README has native-charge-limit warning; MenuBarView conflict with T012 resolved trivially (keep ControlsView + login toggle), merged gate green.

tickets.title: Telemetry sampler (60 s, ring-capped JSONL) + get-stats implementation
tickets.context: Telemetry.swift (AmpereCore, pure-ish, temp-dir testable): TelemetrySample Codable {ts, percent, isCharging, temperatureC, amperageMA, voltageMV, chargingPaused}; TelemetryLog(url:) with append(sample) writing one JSON line, ring-cap at 20,000 lines per SPEC §3 (on exceed: rewrite file keeping the newest 20,000 — do the cap check cheaply, e.g. line counter maintained in memory + on-open count); read(hoursBack:) -> [TelemetrySample] filtering by ts. Daemon wiring: sample every 60 s (reuse the existing 30 s tick, sample on every other tick) to the SPEC §3 path; DaemonServer get-stats(hours) now returns real samples.
tickets.acceptance:
- TelemetryTests in temp dir: append 20,005 samples -> file has exactly 20,000 lines and the OLDEST 5 are gone (assert first line's ts, contrast with pre-cap)
- read(hoursBack: 1) returns only samples newer than 1 h (fixture with old + new ts)
- corrupted line in file -> read skips it without crashing (fixture)
- `swift build` + `swift test` green
tickets.evidence: re-verify 2026-07-06: scope exact (4 files); 104/104 worktree + merged; ring-cap drops OLDEST (test asserts first surviving ts == sample #6), corrupt-line skip, hoursBack filter; 60s cadence via every-other-tick; get-stats serves real samples. Merged; gate green.

tickets.title: Heat protection UI: toggle + threshold stepper + pause-reason surfacing
tickets.context: Heat logic already lives in the control core (T006) and config (T004); this ticket is UI only. In ControlsView: 'Heat protection' Toggle (heatProtectionEnabled) + threshold Stepper 30...45 °C shown when enabled -> set-config; when get-state pauseReason == heat, show a prominent 'Charging paused: battery hot (36.2 °C)' row using live temperatureC. Route through DaemonClientModel as established in T012.

tickets.title: Calibration state machine in control core
tickets.context: Extend ControlCore (T006 built it; its CalibrationState placeholder is nil until now) with the SPEC §3.3 calibration rules: phases discharge(target 15, floor 15: adapter disabled) -> charge(target 100: adapter enabled, charging allowed — heat inhibit still wins per spec) -> hold(1 h from reaching 100, charging allowed) -> done(restore limit mode). Transitions driven by decide()'s now: Date parameter — no timers in core. Abort semantics: calibrate-abort action OR externalConnected going false in any phase -> immediate restore (enableAdapter, limit-mode rules apply next tick). CalibrationState carries {phase, phaseEnteredAt, holdStartedAt?}. Do not change any T006 rule — existing tests must stay green untouched (modifying an existing T006 test = red flag, justify or fail).
tickets.acceptance:
- Swift Testing tests: full happy path via sequential decide() calls with advancing now (percent 60 -> discharge commands disableAdapter -> at 15 flips to charge phase + enableAdapter+allowCharging -> at 100 enters hold with holdStartedAt -> now+61 min -> done, limit mode active again)
- abort from EACH phase (discharge/charge/hold) -> enableAdapter emitted, calibration nil, limit rules resume (three contrast tests)
- unplug during discharge -> same restoration
- heat 36/threshold 35 during charge phase -> inhibitCharging emitted while still calibrating (phase unchanged)
- ALL pre-existing T006 tests pass unmodified
- `swift build` + `swift test` green

tickets.title: Repair: CHTE little-endian encoding + dry-run byte assertion (escaped-bug rule)
tickets.origin: repair: Phase 0 gate red — CHTE write [00 00 00 01] rejected smcResult 137
tickets.context: Phase 0 hardware gate result (docs/smc-findings.md is authoritative — read it): CHIE 0x08/0x00 CONFIRMED working; CHTE rejected big-endian [00 00 00 01] with smcResult 137 while [00 00 00 00] succeeds. OpenDente cross-reference: inhibit = LITTLE-ENDIAN [01 00 00 00]. Fix SpikeCommands: pause writes exactly bytes [0x01,0x00,0x00,0x00] to CHTE; resume writes [0x00,0x00,0x00,0x00]; drop the generic probed-size big-endian encoder for CHTE (keep the CH0B/CH0C fallback as-is: single byte 0x02/0x00). ESCAPED-BUG RULE — also strengthen the check that let this through: add a --dry-run flag to pause/resume/adapter that prints the exact key and byte array that WOULD be written (format: KEY [xx xx xx xx]) and exits 0 without root and without opening SMC. This makes byte encoding verifiable without sudo forever after.
tickets.acceptance:
- .build/debug/ampere-cli pause --dry-run (no sudo) exits 0 printing CHTE [01 00 00 00]
- resume --dry-run prints CHTE [00 00 00 00]
- adapter off --dry-run prints CHIE [08]; adapter on --dry-run prints CHIE [00]
- pause (no --dry-run, no sudo) still exits 1 mentioning root
- swift build + bash scripts/test.sh green (39 tests, no regressions)
tickets.evidence: re-verify 2026-07-05: scope exactly [SpikeCommands.swift]; build exit 0; 39/39; dry-runs print CHTE [01 00 00 00]/[00 00 00 00], CHIE [08]/[00]; root guard intact; dry-run shares chteBytes() with real write path (check cannot drift). Merged; merged gate green.

tickets.title: Repair: socket group must be staff (EACCES for unprivileged clients) + honest connect errors
tickets.origin: repair: Phase 1 [HW] gate red — ampere-cli state errno 13 (EACCES): socket is root:wheel 0660, spec requires root:staff
tickets.context: hw-gate found unprivileged clients cannot connect: daemon (root) binds /var/run/ampere.sock which lands root:wheel 0660; SPEC 3 locks root:staff 0660. The T009/T010 briefs wrongly said ownership happens naturally. Fix: after the socket server starts, the daemon chowns the socket to uid 0 / gid of group staff (getgrnam("staff")?.pointee.gr_gid, fallback gid 20) keeping mode 0660. Also: SocketClient/cli currently report EACCES with a misleading no-socket message - make the error distinguish ENOENT (daemon not running) from EACCES (permission denied - socket group mismatch, suggests reinstalling/restarting daemon).
tickets.acceptance:
- diff shows chown(path, 0, staffGid) (or equivalent) after server start in daemon code, mode still 0660
- connect error for EACCES mentions permission, ENOENT mentions not running (distinct messages)
- swift build + bash scripts/test.sh green (114 must not regress)
- final confirmation is the user re-running scripts/hw-gate.sh
tickets.evidence: re-verify 2026-07-06: scope exact (3 files); 114/114; chown guarded by geteuid()==0 with getgrnam(staff) + gid-20 fallback, re-chmod 0660; ENOENT/EACCES messages distinct — EACCES variant verified LIVE against the machine's actual root:daemon socket. Merged; release rebuilt.

tickets.title: Repair: daemon main-queue deadlock — move state to a dedicated serial queue; non-blocking accept; SIGPIPE guard
tickets.origin: repair: Phase 1 [HW] gate — daemon wedged permanently at 13:00:50 (telemetry heartbeat stopped; all socket requests hang; SIGTERM restore path dead)
tickets.context: Live failure: DaemonServer bridges every request via DispatchQueue.main.sync while Daemon.run() sits in CFRunLoopRun(); timer/signal sources target .main. A client disconnecting mid-request wedged the main queue permanently: control loop dead, socket dead, SIGTERM handler dead (safety rail broken — charging stayed inhibited with no way to restore except SIGKILL+manual CHTE write). Fix architecture: (1) introduce a private serial daemonQueue owning ALL daemon state; timer + signal sources target daemonQueue; socket handler bridges with daemonQueue.sync (plain serial-queue sync from connection queues — no runloop involvement, no main queue anywhere); IOKit callbacks (IOPS notification, IORegisterForSystemPower) still need the CFRunLoop on the main thread — their callbacks must hop to daemonQueue via async and must NOT touch state inline. main.swift keeps CFRunLoopRun() purely as the IOKit source pump. (2) SocketServer: set O_NONBLOCK on the listen fd (and accepted fds), make acceptPending exit on EAGAIN instead of blocking the server queue in accept(). (3) SIGPIPE: signal(SIGPIPE, SIG_IGN) early in daemon startup AND setsockopt SO_NOSIGPIPE on accepted + client fds in SocketServer/SocketClient so a vanished peer can never kill or wedge anything. (4) Sleep/wake IOAllowPowerChange acknowledgment must still happen promptly — keep the acknowledgment on the callback thread, hop only the re-evaluate.
tickets.acceptance:
- new Swift Testing test: server with a handler that responds after 100ms; client connects, sends request, disconnects immediately WITHOUT reading; then a second client sends get-state-style request and gets a response within 2s (regression test for the wedge — this is the exact trigger)
- new test: two concurrent clients each get responses (no serialization deadlock)
- grep contract: no DispatchQueue.main.sync anywhere in Sources/ampered; timer/signal sources target the daemon queue; SIGPIPE ignored; O_NONBLOCK/SO_NOSIGPIPE present in SocketServer
- swift build + bash scripts/test.sh green (114 + new)
- .build/debug/ampered non-root refusal unchanged
tickets.evidence: re-verify 2026-07-06: scope exact (5 files); 116/116 twice; grep contracts: zero DispatchQueue.main in Sources/ampered, O_NONBLOCK+EAGAIN in SocketServer, SIGPIPE guards in server/client/daemon; regression test encodes the live trigger (disconnect-without-reading then next client answered <2s); worker reproduced a real SIGPIPE crash pre-fix and stress-tested 20x post-fix; bonus fd-reuse race in stop() fixed with blocking close. Suite attr change to .serialized judged benign (no test bodies touched). Merged; release rebuilt.

tickets.title: ampere-cli req command + hw-gate.sh drops nc

tickets.context: Add ampere-cli subcommand: req <json-line> — connect via SocketClient, send the line, print exactly one response line, close cleanly, exit 0 (nonzero on connect/timeout error; reuse the T022 error messages). Then rewrite scripts/hw-gate.sh req() to use  req instead of nc (remove the nc dependency entirely). Behaviour of the rest of the gate unchanged.

- bash -n scripts/hw-gate.sh clean; grep shows no nc usage remains

tickets.title: Repair: self-induced-unplug suppression (discharge/calibration self-abort) + explicit calibration null + gate discharge check
tickets.origin: repair: Phase 4 [HW] gate red — calibrate-start self-aborts <1s (SPEC design flaw: our own adapter-off reads as externalConnected==false and trips the unplug-abort). SEMANTIC oracle amendment user-approved 2026-07-06, SPEC §3.3 updated.
tickets.context: Diagnosis (fully verified): decide() preserves calibration, protocol decodes calibrate-start correctly, payload encodes calibration when present — but disabling the adapter makes macOS report externalConnected=false, which SPEC pre-amendment rules treated as unplug -> instant self-abort of calibration AND self-cancel of the discharge-to-limit one-shot. Fix per amended SPEC §3.3 (read it): (1) ControlCore: track adapter assertion via lastCommands.contains(.disableAdapter) and an adapterEnabledAt: Date? stamped when enableAdapter is emitted; when adapter is asserted off OR within 10s of re-enable (settle window), externalConnected==false does NOT cancel the discharge one-shot and does NOT abort calibration — the active mode keeps driving (floors 20/15 remain). Existing tests that contradict the amended rule (unplug-cancels tests whose state has adapter asserted off) may be UPDATED — list each changed test in your report with justification; tests where adapter was NOT asserted (true unplug while plugged-in state) must stay green unmodified. (2) Protocol.swift: GetStatePayload custom encode must emit explicit "calibration":null when nil (SPEC §3.1 shows explicit null; currently the key is omitted) — extend ProtocolTests with a contrast (nil -> "calibration":null present in JSON; non-nil -> object). (3) scripts/hw-gate.sh: add a discharge-to-limit check before the calibration section: with limit 80 and battery >80, send action discharge-to-limit, within 15s expect get-state adapterDisabled:true AND pmset shows Battery Power; then set-limit to current percent (completes the one-shot immediately), within 15s expect adapterDisabled:false and AC Power restored; restore limit 80. Keep existing calibration greps (explicit null now makes them correct).
tickets.acceptance:
- new ControlCore contrast tests: (a) calibration discharge phase + externalConnected false + lastCommands contains disableAdapter -> calibration SURVIVES and keeps disableAdapter; (b) same but adapter NOT asserted (genuine unplug) -> aborts as before; (c) discharge one-shot analog of both; (d) settle window: enableAdapter emitted at t, externalConnected false at t+5s -> no abort; at t+15s still false -> abort
- ProtocolTests: calibration nil encodes literal "calibration":null (string assert), non-nil encodes object (contrast)
- bash -n scripts/hw-gate.sh clean; discharge check present
- swift build + bash scripts/test.sh green (116 baseline; only amendment-contradicting tests changed, each listed)
tickets.evidence: re-verify 2026-07-06: scope exact (5 files); 121/121 twice; suppression logic per amended SPEC §3.3 (lastCommands assertion + adapterEnabledAt 10s settle window, stamped in emitAdapter); encodeNil for calibration; gate discharge check present, bash -n clean; 3 pre-existing tests modified, all three contradicted the amendment and each genuine-unplug variant preserved as a separate test with original expectations. Merged; release rebuilt.

tickets.title: Repair: accepted sockets inherit O_NONBLOCK (macOS/BSD) — large responses (get-stats) dropped mid-write
tickets.origin: repair: live dashboard red — get-stats (~60KB) gets connection closed by server; small responses fine
tickets.context: T023 set O_NONBLOCK on the LISTEN fd. On macOS (BSD semantics) accepted connections INHERIT non-blocking mode, and Connection.writeAll handles only EINTR — the first write() returning EAGAIN once the ~8KB socket buffer fills is treated as fatal -> closeConnection. T023 itself documented the intent to keep accepted fds blocking. Fix: in acceptPending(), immediately after accept() returns clientFD, explicitly CLEAR O_NONBLOCK on clientFD (fcntl F_GETFL, then F_SETFL flags & ~O_NONBLOCK) with a comment explaining BSD inheritance. Reads stay DispatchSourceRead-driven (fire only when data is ready, so blocking reads are safe); writes then block until drained, which per T023 blocks only that connection queue (acceptable, documented). Do NOT add EAGAIN retry loops to writeAll — restoring blocking semantics is the fix.
tickets.acceptance:
- new Swift Testing regression test: handler returns a >200KB response line; client sends request and receives the ENTIRE line intact (assert length + last-char marker) — this fails before the fix, passes after
- existing 121 tests green, unmodified (append-only on the test file)
- grep: fcntl clearing O_NONBLOCK present in acceptPending with the BSD-inheritance comment
- swift build + bash scripts/test.sh green
tickets.evidence: re-verify 2026-07-06: scope exact (2 files); append-only test diff (0 deletions); 122/122; red/green check performed by builder (red reproduced the exact live "connection closed by server" at 250KB, green x2); fcntl clears inherited O_NONBLOCK at accept with BSD-inheritance comment; listen fd untouched. Merged; release rebuilt.

tickets.title: Protocol deltas: StatsSample.chargingPaused, GetStatePayload.adapter, AdapterDetails parser
tickets.context:
SPEC §9.3/§9.4 (locked, additive-only; also mirrored in .ailoop/oracle.md Phase 5). Three changes:
1. `StatsSample` (Sources/AmpereCore/Protocol.swift ~line 303) gains `chargingPaused: Bool`. It already has a hand-rolled `init(from:)` that defaults `amperageMA`/`voltageMV` to 0 when absent — follow exactly that pattern: encode always, decode with default `false` (old-daemon compat). Add a pure mapping in AmpereCore, `StatsSample.init(_ sample: TelemetrySample)` (or `static func from(_:)`), that copies ts/percent/isCharging/temperatureC/amperageMA/voltageMV/chargingPaused — and change `Daemon.getStats(hours:)` (Sources/ampered/Daemon.swift ~line 554) to use it. The mapping MUST live in AmpereCore so it is unit-testable (ampered is an executable target, not importable from tests); the daemon must call it (hand-rolled duplicate mapping in Daemon.swift = fail).
2. New `AdapterPayload: Codable, Equatable, Sendable { watts: Int, name: String? }` in Protocol.swift. `GetStatePayload` gains `adapter: AdapterPayload?` — decode `nil` when absent. NOTE: GetStatePayload has custom Codable (it encodes `"calibration":null` explicitly); keep its existing encoding conventions intact and add `adapter` without breaking them (omit-or-null when nil is fine, must round-trip).
3. `BatteryReader` (Sources/AmpereCore/BatteryReader.swift) parses the `AdapterDetails` sub-dictionary (`[String: Any]`) of the same injected AppleSmartBattery dict: `Watts` (Int, required), `Name` else `Description` (String, optional). Pure and total like the rest of the parser: absent dict, mistyped `Watts`, or missing watts → `nil` adapter; never crash, never invent values. Expose it on the reader's parsed result; `Daemon` populates `GetStatePayload.adapter` from it, `nil` when `externalConnected == false`. AdapterDetails is a READ — Phase 5 writes zero SMC keys (tripwire).
tickets.acceptance:
Baseline (oracle.md): `swift build` exit 0 AND `bash scripts/test.sh` all green (NEVER plain `swift test` — CLT-only machine). New behavior ships with new Swift Testing tests.
- ProtocolTests: (a) StatsSample JSON WITHOUT `chargingPaused` decodes to `false`; (b) encode always emits the key; (c) CONTRAST: two TelemetrySamples identical except `chargingPaused` map (via the new AmpereCore mapping) to wire samples differing exactly in `chargingPaused` — a hardcoded value cannot pass both.
- ProtocolTests: GetStatePayload JSON without `adapter` decodes `nil`; with adapter round-trips (watts + name, and watts-only).
- BatteryReaderTests, all four: `{Watts: 96, Name: "96W USB-C Power Adapter"}` → payload(96, name); AdapterDetails absent → nil; `Watts` mistyped (e.g. String "96") → nil; `Name`+`Description` absent → payload(96, nil).
- `swift build` proves Daemon.swift compiles against the new shapes.
tickets.evidence: independent re-verify on worktree + merged tree: swift build exit 0; scripts/test.sh 133/133 green (was 122 → +11 new tests: 6 ProtocolTests incl. contrast statsSampleFromTelemetrySampleMapsChargingPausedAndNothingElseDiffers, 5 BatteryReaderTests covering all four parser cases + Description fallback). Scope diff exactly the 5 contracted files. Gaming read clean: mapping in AmpereCore, Daemon.getStats uses .map(StatsSample.init) (hand-rolled copy removed); encode always emits chargingPaused, decodeIfPresent??false; adapter encodeIfPresent, gated on externalConnected. Deviation (accepted): adapter exposed via parseAdapter/readLiveAdapter + injectable Daemon.AdapterReader closure instead of widening frozen BatteryReading.

tickets.title: Telemetry archive: downsample-on-rotate, 40k ring, get-stats hours:0 merge
tickets.context:
SPEC §9.2 (locked shape) + §9.3 merge semantics. In Sources/AmpereCore/Telemetry.swift:
1. `ArchiveSample: Codable, Equatable, Sendable` exactly per §9.2: ts (15-min-aligned bucket start), percentAvg/percentMin/percentMax, temperatureCAvg, amperageMAAvg, voltageMVAvg, chargingFraction, pausedFraction, count. ISO8601 dates like TelemetrySample.
2. Pure, unit-testable `func bucket(_ samples: [TelemetrySample]) -> [ArchiveSample]` — bucket key floor(ts/900s), averages are arithmetic means, fractions = (matching count)/count.
3. Rotation hook: `TelemetryLog` gains an archive `URL` (default: sibling `telemetry-archive.jsonl`; init parameter so tests use temp dirs). In `append(_:)`'s existing ring-rewrite branch (the `count >= capLines` path), the lines being DROPPED (the ones not in `newest`) are decoded, bucketed via `bucket(...)`, and appended to the archive BEFORE the hot rewrite. Archive has its own ring: `archiveCapLines` default 40_000, same rewrite-when-exceeded drop-oldest mechanism. Corrupt dropped lines are skipped (matching `read`'s tolerance). Add `readArchive() -> [ArchiveSample]`.
4. Merge (pure, in Telemetry.swift or StatsFormatting.swift): `func mergedStats(archive: [ArchiveSample], hot: [TelemetrySample], hoursBack: Double) -> [StatsSample]` — hoursBack 0 means ALL history; archive buckets strictly older than the oldest hot sample map to StatsSample (percent = rounded percentAvg, isCharging = chargingFraction >= 0.5, chargingPaused = pausedFraction >= 0.5, averages copied; uses T027's chargingPaused field), then hot samples via T027's mapping; chronological; finally `StatsFormatting.downsample(_, to: 2000)` server-side cap.
5. `Daemon.getStats(hours:)` (Sources/ampered/Daemon.swift): hours 0 = all; answer via `mergedStats`. Existing positive-hours behavior must keep passing whatever covers it today. Wire shape of get-stats is UNCHANGED (additive semantics only).
tickets.acceptance:
Baseline (oracle.md): `swift build` exit 0 AND `bash scripts/test.sh` all green (NEVER plain `swift test` — CLT-only machine). New behavior ships with new Swift Testing tests.
- TelemetryTests bucketing CONTRAST: samples spanning two 15-min windows → exactly 2 buckets; assert NUMERIC percentAvg/percentMin/percentMax, temperatureCAvg, count, and pausedFraction (e.g. 5 of 15 paused → 1.0/3.0 ±0.001) — bucket-count-only assertions are insufficient.
- TelemetryTests rotation: TelemetryLog(capLines: 10, archive in temp dir) driven with 25 timestamped samples → hot file ≤ 10 lines AND readArchive() buckets cover the dropped samples' time range (assert on bucket ts + count), not merely non-empty.
- TelemetryTests archive ring: small archiveCapLines exceeded → oldest archive lines dropped, cap held.
- Merge tests: (a) hoursBack 0 with >2,000 combined inputs → ≤2,000 chronological samples containing both archive-era and hot-era timestamps; (b) mapping CONTRAST: pausedFraction 0.6 → chargingPaused true, 0.4 → false (same for chargingFraction/isCharging).
- Existing telemetry ring-cap test (Phase 3 oracle) still green — regression guard on the rewrite path.
tickets.evidence: independent re-verify on worktree + merged tree: swift build exit 0; scripts/test.sh 144/144 (was 133; +11: 4 TelemetryTests incl. rotation coverage test attributing archive buckets to specifically-dropped samples, 7 StatsFormattingTests incl. threshold contrasts 0.6→true/0.4→false for both fractions). Scope diff exactly the 5 contracted files. Gaming read clean: rotation hook archives dropped lines before hot rewrite; archive has own 40k ring; read(hoursBack:) contract preserved via new readAll(); mergedStats implements strictly-older-than-oldest-hot rule + downsample 2000; Daemon.getStats hours==0 → readAll+readArchive+mergedStats.

tickets.title: Pure derived logic: time-to-limit estimate + charge session log
tickets.context:
SPEC §9.5 (locked formulas). NEW file Sources/AmpereCore/StatsDerived.swift, pure functions only, no IOKit, no Foundation beyond Date math.
1. `struct TimeEstimate { minutes: Int, targetPercent: Int }` (or equivalent) and `func timeEstimate(samples: [StatsSample], percent: Int, limit: Int, mode: String, sailingEnabled: Bool, sailingOffset: Int, maxCapacityMAh: Int, now: Date) -> TimeEstimate?` — exact signature may vary but inputs must be these values (they come from GetStatePayload + Config; do NOT take the whole payload if that couples the API awkwardly — builder's choice, but pure). Rate = mean amperageMA of the newest ≤ 10 samples with ts within 15 min of `now`. |rate| < 50 mA → nil. Charging (rate > 0): target = limit, or 100 when mode is topping-up or calibrating(charge). Discharging: target = limit − 5, or limit − sailingOffset when sailing; 20 during discharge-to-limit mode; 15 during calibration discharge. minutes = (targetPercent − percent)/100 × maxCapacityMAh / |rate| × 60, rounded. Already past target (sign says you'd move away) → nil.
2. `enum SessionKind { charging, holding, discharging, idle }`, `struct ChargeSession { kind, start, end, fromPercent, toPercent }`, `func sessions(from samples: [StatsSample]) -> [ChargeSession]`. Per-sample classification precedence: isCharging → charging; chargingPaused && !isCharging → holding; amperageMA <= -50 (and not holding) → discharging; else idle. Merge consecutive same-kind runs; a gap > 5 min between adjacent samples closes the current run; drop runs shorter than 5 min. Uses T027's StatsSample.chargingPaused.
tickets.acceptance:
Baseline (oracle.md): `swift build` exit 0 AND `bash scripts/test.sh` all green (NEVER plain `swift test` — CLT-only machine). New behavior ships with new Swift Testing tests.
- timeEstimate CONTRAST: two fixtures differing only in amperage (e.g. +1000 mA vs +2000 mA, percent 60, limit 80, maxCapacity 5199) → two DIFFERENT minute values each asserted EXACTLY against the §9.5 formula (≈62 and ≈31 min) — a non-nil-only check is insufficient.
- |mean rate| < 50 mA → nil; percent already at/above target while charging → nil; samples older than 15 min excluded from the rate window (fixture proves it).
- discharging + sailingEnabled(offset 8, limit 80) → targetPercent 72; discharge-to-limit mode → target 20.
- sessions: gap > 5 min splits an otherwise-identical run into two; a 4-min run is dropped, a 6-min run kept; classification contrast fixture with all four kinds; merged run's fromPercent/toPercent = first/last sample's percent.
tickets.evidence: independent re-verify on worktree + merged tree: swift build exit 0; scripts/test.sh 163/163 (was 144; +19 StatsDerivedTests). Scope: exactly the 2 new contract files. Gaming read clean: rate window (15 min, newest ≤10, |mean|≥50), target-selection precedence exactly per §9.5 (calibrating-discharge 15 > discharging 20 > sailing limit−offset > limit−5; topping-up/calibrating-charge → 100), direction guard returns nil at/past target; classification precedence charging>holding>discharging>idle; contrast test asserts exact 62 vs 31 min from the formula.

tickets.title: Dashboard UI rework: scrolling layout, live refresh, range picker, 3 charts + shading, sessions
tickets.context:
SPEC §9.6 (locked layout + mechanisms). Rework Sources/Ampere/StatsView.swift (keep StatsWindowPresenter's single retained NSWindow; default size 480×640, min 440×480):
- Single ScrollView layout, top→bottom: existing 4 tiles (Health/Cycles/Temp/Power, semantics unchanged) → live detail rows: voltage `%.2f V` + amperage signed mA (from newest sample), charger row from GetStatePayload.adapter (T027: "96W USB-C Power Adapter" style when present, "No charger" when nil), time-to-limit line from StatsDerived.timeEstimate (T029; hidden when nil) → segmented range picker 24 h/7 d/30 d/All → hours 24/168/720/0 (T028 merge semantics) → three Swift Charts (battery % y-domain 0…100, temperature, power W = amperageMA×voltageMV/1e6) → session list (StatsDerived.sessions, newest ≤ 20, rows like "Held at 80% — 3 h 12 m").
- Paused shading: contiguous chargingPaused==true runs → RectangleMark x-spans BEHIND the LineMark on the % and power charts, low-opacity accent fill. Extract the runs via a PURE helper `pausedIntervals(_ samples: [StatsSample]) -> [(start: Date, end: Date)]` in Sources/AmpereCore/StatsFormatting.swift (testable; the view only renders it). Same file gets `rangeHours` mapping + session-row/time-estimate string formatters if not already pure.
- Client downsample ≤ 400 points per chart after range selection (reuse StatsFormatting.downsample).
- Live refresh (locked mechanism): two Timers owned by the view — 5 s tick re-fetches get-state, 60 s tick re-fetches get-stats for the selected range. Each tick NO-OPS unless the hosting window `isVisible` (no teardown races with the retained window). Range change → immediate re-fetch. Keep the manual Refresh button (forces both).
- Tripwires (SPEC §9.9): no export, no zoom/pan/annotations, no extra charts, no menu-bar changes.
- UI acceptance is deliberately thin here: everything judgeable without a human eye must live in the pure helpers; the visual checks are the phase [HW] gate (oracle.md Phase 5), NOT this ticket.
